aiAssistant.title: AI Assistant
aiAssistant.description: Configure the provider, model, and instructions that guide each automatic reply.
aiAssistant.enabledHint: Automatically reply to incoming messages using your AI provider.
aiAssistant.apiKeyHint: Stored encrypted and never shown. Leave empty to keep it unchanged.
aiAssistant.modelHint: Models available for your API key. Refresh the list if you added a new key.
aiAssistant.modelHintNoKey: Add and save your API key to fetch the available models.
aiAssistant.modelsEmpty: Couldn't fetch models. Check that the API key is valid.
aiAssistant.systemPromptPlaceholder: You are a customer support assistant…
aiAssistant.systemPromptHint: Define tone, limits, data to request, and when to hand off to the team. Up to {{max}} characters.
aiAssistant.templateLimit: There is not enough space to add the template.
aiAssistant.promptEmpty: Without instructions, AI replies only with the available context.
aiAssistant.promptReady: These instructions are sent as fixed context with each reply.
aiAssistant.promptTemplate:
You are the customer support assistant for {{tenant_name}}.

Goal:
- Help each contact with clear, brief, actionable replies.
- Ask for missing information before promising a solution.
- Hand off to a team member when there are doubts, sensitive complaints, or advanced purchase intent.

Tone:
- Professional, warm, and direct.
- Do not invent prices, stock, timelines, or policies.

Rules:
- Reply in the contact's language.
- Use the conversation context before repeating questions.
- If you do not have enough information, explain which detail you need.
aiAssistant.testOk: Connection successful. The API key works.
aiAssistant.testCacheOk: Above the cache minimum ({{min}} tokens): prompt caching applies.
aiAssistant.testCacheBelow: The system prompt ({{tokens}} tokens) is below the cache minimum ({{min}}): no caching.
aiAssistant.testErrorNoCredit: The provider account has insufficient balance.
aiAssistant.testErrorRateLimit: The provider is rate limiting requests. Try again in a few seconds.

webhooks.signingSecretHint: If set, every request must include the HMAC-SHA256 signature in the X-Signature-256 header. Stored encrypted.
webhooks.empty: Create the first one to receive contacts from your system. You will get a URL and an API key to start sending.
webhooks.rotateConfirm: A new key will be generated for "{{name}}" and the current one will stop working instantly. Update the system that uses it as soon as you copy it.
webhooks.deleteConfirm: Delete endpoint "{{name}}"? Its API key will stop working.
webhooks.keyWarning: Save it now: for security it won't be shown again. If you lose it, you can rotate it.
webhooks.deliveriesEmpty: No requests received on this endpoint yet.
webhooks.processingDetail: The batch is being processed. The result will show up here when it finishes.
webhooks.processingDetailCount: Processing {{count}} contacts. The result will show up here when it finishes.

payload.title: Request format
payload.description: Authentication and the JSON body are the same for both variants; the URL and how you receive the result change.
payload.variantSyncDetail: Up to 500 contacts per request. The response includes the upsert result.
payload.variantBulkDetail: Up to 5,000 contacts per request. Responds 202 instantly, processes in the background, and the status is polled via GET.
payload.exampleSyncDescription: One POST, result in the same response. Ideal for syncing contacts as they change in your system.
payload.exampleBulkDescription: The 202 returns delivery_id and status_url. Poll that URL until you see processed, partial or failed. Retrying a batch is safe: repeated external_id values are updated, not duplicated.
payload.authenticationDescription: Send a POST request to the endpoint's public URL and include these headers. The API key identifies the endpoint and the tenant that will receive the contacts.
payload.apiKeyDescription: Required on every request. Send the API key in the header, never inside the JSON body or URL:
payload.contentTypeDescription: Specifies that the request body uses JSON:
payload.signatureDescription: Only required when a signing secret is configured. It contains the HMAC-SHA256 signature of the exact request body.
payload.apiKeyWarning: The complete API key is shown only when created or rotated. If you lose it, rotate it: the previous credential will stop working.
payload.noCustomFields: This tenant has no custom fields yet. The example updates automatically when you add one.

fields.contacts: A list of contacts: up to 500 in real time, up to 5,000 in bulk.
fields.externalId: A stable identifier for the contact in your system.
fields.name: Full name, up to 255 characters.
fields.customData: An object using the keys configured for this tenant, listed below.

payload.upsertDescription: If external_id already exists, the contact is updated. Fields you omit keep their current value.

businessVerification.title: Business verification (Meta)
businessVerification.subtitle: Meta business verification status for each connected WhatsApp number.

howTo.trigger: How do I verify my business?
howTo.intro: Verification is completed in Meta's Business Manager, not in the CRM. Follow these steps:
howTo.step1: Click "Verify on Meta": your business Security Center opens on Meta.
howTo.step2: Under "Business verification", start the process and fill in your company's legal details (legal name, address, phone).
howTo.step3: Upload the documentation Meta requests (tax certificate, utility bill or another proof in the business name).
howTo.step4: Submit the request and wait for Meta's review. It usually takes a few days; the status here updates when you click "Refresh".
howTo.note: You must be an admin of the Business Manager on Meta to verify.
